From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 14:02 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 15:56 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 11:14 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-01-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:01:15] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is competitive positioning.

[00:06:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:09:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:16:00] Priya (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:20:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:23:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:27:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:31:00] Priya (VanArsdel Ltd): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.
